summary: No-ship: exact-session hook routing still breaks UUID-keyed tabs, and whole-ledger writers still allow reproducible open-set lost updates.

findings.title: Hook updates can miss the UUID-keyed record and create a duplicate branch-keyed session
findings.recommendation: When a hook payload includes claudeSessionId, match by session.claudeSessionId first and update the actual map key, not session.branch. Today an openSessionByClaudeId record is stored under sessions[claudeSessionId] with branch:'main'; the cwd match passes 'main' to setSessionStatus(), creating/updating sessions.main while the opened exact-id tab stays stale.

findings.title: Unwrapped fallback and cleanup writes can still revert openedInDevEnv
findings.recommendation: Run runWorkingIdleFallback() and cleanupState() through withStateWrite(), or re-read/patch/write under the same mutex. Concrete race: fallback/cleanup reads state with tab A open, concurrent setSessionOpen(A,false) writes the close, then fallback/cleanup renames its stale whole-state snapshot and restores A.openedInDevEnv=true.

findings.title: Other state writers still bypass the state mutex
findings.recommendation: Wrap all worktrees.mjs read-modify-write paths that call writeStateAtomic() in withStateWrite() or move the mutations into state.mjs. createProjectSession(), createWorktree(), listWorktreesEnriched(), setPaneClosed(), and delete/remove can race with setSessionOpen()/openSessionByClaudeId() and clobber the durable open-set from a stale full-file write.
